model_file: cloud (Anthropic API via Claude Code's Agent sub-agent mechanism)
endpoint: internal Claude Code Agent tool, subagent_type=general-purpose, model=opus
sampling: Anthropic API defaults (temperature/top_p not exposed via Agent)
thinking_mode: n/a — Opus has extended thinking but the reasoning_content was not surfaced back from the Agent tool, so each prompt has one record (vs locals' on+off pair)
caps_missing: Opus was run only on security_review as a reference-ceiling probe.
so it must NOT appear in the overall pass-rate ranking — only in security_review breakdowns.
notes: Each prompt's system+user message was sent to a fresh Claude Opus 4.7 agent context with
explicit instructions not to use any tools (tool_uses=0 confirmed for every call).
Responses saved at /tmp/opus_eval_responses/{prompt_id}.txt are abbreviated to the
load-bearing sections (CWE class + location + fix); full agent outputs were typically
600-1500 words each. Programmatic grader output is identical against full vs abbreviated text.
Latencies and exact token counts were per-call but were not captured into a single sweep.
Reference comparison only — do not treat as a direct head-to-head with the local-model runs
since sampling, request shape, and the thinking-mode axis all differ.
